channel.title: NPR Topics: World
channel.description: NPR world news, international art and culture, world business and financial markets, world economy, and global trends in health, science and technology. Subscribe to the World Story of the Day podcast and RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Air Force says former Qatari 747 will be ready to fly as Air Force One this summer
item.description: The U.S. Air Force has finished modifying and testing a Boeing 747 jet donated by Qatar for temporary use as Air Force One and expects to have it ready for President Trump to use this summer.
item.content:encoded:
The U.S. Air Force has finished modifying and testing a Boeing 747 jet donated by Qatar for temporary use as Air Force One and expects to have it ready for President Trump to use this summer.

(Image credit: Matt Rourke)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: U.S. to withdraw 5,000 troops from Germany in next 6-12 months
item.description: The United States will withdraw about 5,000 troops from Germany, the Pentagon said Friday, fulfilling President Donald Trump's threat as he clashes with the German leader over the U.S. war with Iran.
item.content:encoded:
The United States will withdraw about 5,000 troops from Germany, the Pentagon said Friday, fulfilling President Donald Trump's threat as he clashes with the German leader over the U.S. war with Iran.

(Image credit: Matt Rourke)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: After Canada loosens citizenship requirements, thousands of people apply
item.description: After Canada changed a law opening up citizenship opportunities, thousands of Americans are trying to become citizens of their northern neighbor. It's partly for political and personal reasons.
item.content:encoded: After Canada changed a law opening up citizenship opportunities, thousands of Americans are trying to become citizens of their northern neighbor. It's partly for political and personal reasons.
item.dc:creator: Ari Snider

item.title: Record-breaking DHS shutdown ends. And, May Day protests to draw crowds nationwide
item.description: Congress has ended the record-breaking shutdown of the Department of Homeland Security. And, May Day demonstrations across the U.S. are expected to draw crowds protesting the Trump administration.
item.content:encoded:
Congress has ended the record-breaking shutdown of the Department of Homeland Security. And, May Day demonstrations across the U.S. are expected to draw crowds protesting the Trump administration.

(Image credit: Graeme Sloan)
item.dc:creator: Brittney Melton

item.title: A tech worker in China is laid off and replaced by AI. Is it legal?
item.description: A tech worker in eastern China's Hangzhou city was dismissed after his job was replaced by AI. An appeals court in the city has ruled the dismissal unlawful.
item.content:encoded:
A tech worker in eastern China's Hangzhou city was dismissed after his job was replaced by AI. An appeals court in the city has ruled the dismissal unlawful.

(Image credit: Andy Wong)
item.dc:creator: Jennifer Pak

item.title: 'Decimate' means much more today than it did in ancient Rome
item.description: Today, most people know the word as a synonym for "destroy." But fewer realize its origins — or that it's come to mean something strikingly different than it once did.
item.content:encoded:
Today, most people know the word as a synonym for "destroy." But fewer realize its origins — or that it's come to mean something strikingly different than it once did.

(Image credit: William Hogarth)
item.dc:creator: Scott Neuman

item.title: The U.S. once had a monopoly on rare earths. How it lost to China
item.description: Planet Money tells the story of how the U.S. lost to China on rare earths.
item.content:encoded: Planet Money tells the story of how the U.S. lost to China on rare earths.
item.dc:creator: Emily Feng

item.title: Myanmar attempts to rehabilitate image with Suu Kyi move
item.description: State television in Myanmar says detained opposition leader Aung San Suu Kyi has been transferred from prison to house arrest, more than five years after the military coup that removed her from power.
item.content:encoded:
State television in Myanmar says detained opposition leader Aung San Suu Kyi has been transferred from prison to house arrest, more than five years after the military coup that removed her from power.

(Image credit: Myanmar Military True News Information Team)
item.dc:creator: Michael Sullivan

item.title: Man charged with attempted murder after stabbings of Jewish men in London
item.description: A 45-year-old man was charged with attempted murder in the stabbings of two Jewish men in London, the latest in a string of attacks that have sparked fear and anger in Britain's Jewish community.
item.content:encoded:
A 45-year-old man was charged with attempted murder in the stabbings of two Jewish men in London, the latest in a string of attacks that have sparked fear and anger in Britain's Jewish community.

(Image credit: Alastair Grant)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Hegseth faces questions about Iran in first congressional appearance since war began
item.description: Making his first appearance before Congress since the Trump administration went to war against Iran, Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth faced withering questioning from skeptical Democrats.
item.content:encoded:
Making his first appearance before Congress since the Trump administration went to war against Iran, Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth faced withering questioning from skeptical Democrats.

(Image credit: Rod Lamkey Jr.)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press
